01.30.26 JUST Congruency Prelim/Power Analyses for As-predicted

Setup

Code

library(reticulate)

use_condaenv("hssm_venv", required = TRUE)

import numpy as np

Code 
#
# LOAD IN SIMULATED DATA 
#----------------
#
cong = pd.read_csv('simed_data_02.18.26_mood_congruency.csv')

cong_100 = pd.read_csv('simed_data_100sims_02.18.26_congruency.csv')
Code 
### See what dfs look like

# Pull dfs from python
cong <- py$cong

cong_100 <- py$cong_100

#---Glimpse what congruency df looks like---#
describe(cong)
Code 
#skim(cong)

Data Prep

Code 
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PREP DATA
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prep consists of:
# 1) filter to include only POSNEG trials
# 2) make chose pos var (0 = neg choice, 1 = pos choice)
# 3) z score RT within pts
# 4) bin affect and pos-neg self belief for plotting
# 5) renamed some vars so looks cleaner


#---PREP CONGRUENCY---#
df_cong <- cong %>%
    filter(delta_valence != 0) %>%
    mutate(
        chose_positive = as.integer(
            (delta_valence == -1 & response_0_1 == 0) | # POSNEG trial (rightside negword leftside pos word)
                (delta_valence == 1 & response_0_1 == 1) # NEGPOS trial (rightside posword leftside neg word)
        ),
        affect_z = affect_z_emalevel,
        delta_sb = delta_self_belief,
        affect_bin = cut(affect_z,
            breaks = c(-Inf, -0.5, 0.5, Inf),
            labels = c("Unpleasant", "Neutral", "Pleasant")
        ),
        delta_posneg_sb = case_when(
            delta_valence == 1 ~ delta_sb, # NEGPOS trial (rightside posword leftside neg word)
            delta_valence == -1 ~ -delta_sb # POSNEG trial (rightside negword leftside pos word)
        ),
        delta_sb_bin = cut(delta_posneg_sb,
            breaks = seq(-0.5, 0.5, by = 0.1),
            labels = c(
                "-100 to -80", "-80 to -60", "-60 to -40", "-40 to -20", "-20 to 0",
                "0 to 20", "20 to 40", "40 to 60", "60 to 80", "80 to 100"
            ),
            include.lowest = TRUE
        )
    ) %>%
      group_by(subject_id) %>%
      mutate(rt_z_within = as.numeric(scale(rt))) %>%
      mutate(delta_posneg_sb_z = as.numeric(scale(delta_posneg_sb))) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(rt_z = as.numeric(scale(rt)))

#cat("df_cong:", nrow(df_cong), "trials,", length(unique(df_cong$subject_id)), "subjects")

01.14.26 Create orthogonal polynomials for regular dfs

Code 
poly_fn <- function(df) {
  poly_result <- poly(df$delta_posneg_sb, degree = 2)
  #browser()  
  df$delta_posneg_sb_lin <- poly_result[, 1]
  #browser()  
  df$delta_posneg_sb_quad <- poly_result[, 2]
  #browser()  
  return(df)
}

df_cong_split <- split(df_cong, df_cong$subject_id)
df_cong <- bind_rows(lapply(df_cong_split, poly_fn))

Data Prep (for *100 sim dfs)

Code 
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PREP *100 SIM DATA
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#---PREP CONGRUENCY---#
df_cong_100 <- cong_100 %>%
    filter(delta_valence != 0) %>%
    mutate(
        chose_positive = as.integer(
            (delta_valence == -1 & response_0_1 == 0) |
                (delta_valence == 1 & response_0_1 == 1)
        ),
        affect_z = affect_z_emalevel,
        delta_sb = delta_self_belief,
        affect_bin = cut(affect_z,
            breaks = c(-Inf, -0.5, 0.5, Inf),
            labels = c("Unpleasant", "Neutral", "Pleasant")
        ),
        delta_posneg_sb = case_when(
            delta_valence == 1 ~ delta_sb,
            delta_valence == -1 ~ -delta_sb
        ),
        delta_sb_bin = cut(delta_posneg_sb,
            breaks = seq(-0.5, 0.5, by = 0.1),
            labels = c(
                "-100 to -80", "-80 to -60", "-60 to -40", "-40 to -20", "-20 to 0",
                "0 to 20", "20 to 40", "40 to 60", "60 to 80", "80 to 100"
            ),
            include.lowest = TRUE
        )
    ) %>%
      group_by(subject_id, experiment_num) %>%
      mutate(delta_posneg_sb_z = as.numeric(scale(delta_posneg_sb))) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(rt_z = as.numeric(scale(rt)))

01.14.26 Create orthogonal polynomials for *100 dfs

Code 
df_cong_100 <- df_cong_100 |>
  mutate(subj_exp = paste(subject_id, experiment_num, sep = "_"))

df_cong_100_split <- split(df_cong_100, df_cong_100$subj_exp)

poly_fn_exp <- function(df) {
  subj <- unique(df$subject_id)
  exp_num <- unique(df$experiment_num)
  poly_result <- poly(df$delta_posneg_sb, degree = 2)
  #browser()
  df$delta_posneg_sb_lin <- poly_result[, 1]
  df$delta_posneg_sb_quad <- poly_result[, 2]
  return(df)
}

df_cong_100 <- bind_rows(lapply(df_cong_100_split, poly_fn_exp))

EMA count

Code 
ema_counts <- df_cong_100 %>%
  group_by(subject_id, experiment_num) %>%
  summarise(n_emas = n_distinct(ema_num), .groups = "drop")

summary(ema_counts$n_emas)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
  25.00   28.00   30.00   28.71   30.00   30.00

PEEK AT SDs

Code 
df_cong %>%
  group_by(subject_id) %>%
  summarise(
    sd_rt_z = sd(rt_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_affect = sd(affect_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_delta_sb_lin = sd(delta_posneg_sb_lin, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_delta_sb_quad = sd(delta_posneg_sb_quad, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_within_sd_rt = mean(sd_rt_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_within_sd_affect = mean(sd_affect, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_within_sd_delta_lin = mean(sd_delta_sb_lin, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_within_sd_delta_quad = mean(sd_delta_sb_quad, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
Code 
df_cong %>%
  group_by(subject_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_affect = mean(affect_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_lin = mean(delta_posneg_sb_lin, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_quad = mean(delta_posneg_sb_quad, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    between_sd_rt = sd(mean_rt),
    between_sd_affect = sd(mean_affect),
    between_sd_delta_lin = sd(mean_delta_lin),
    between_sd_delta_quad = sd(mean_delta_quad)
  )
Code 
means <- df_cong %>%
  group_by(subject_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rt_z_within = mean(rt_z_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rt_z = mean(rt_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_affect = mean(affect_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_lin = mean(delta_posneg_sb_lin, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_quad = mean(delta_posneg_sb_quad, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

means
Code 
ema_means <- df_cong %>%
  group_by(subject_id,ema_num) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rt_z_within = mean(rt_z_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rt_z = mean(rt_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_affect = mean(affect_z, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_lin = mean(delta_posneg_sb_lin, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_quad = mean(delta_posneg_sb_quad, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

hist(df_cong$rt_z_within)

hist(df_cong$rt_z)
Code 
hist(df_cong$rt)

————————————————————————————————

H1a. MOOD CONGRUENCY

————————————————————————————————

PREDICTIONS EXPLAINED QUALITATIVELY

Choice Predictions:

- When affective valence becomes more pleasant participants will choose a higher  pchose pos than when affect is at its average value.
    
- When affective valence becomes more unpleasant participants will have lower pchose pos  than when affect is at its average value.

RT Predictions:

- PLEASANT
    - When pts are deciding between posneg wordpair pair  where the NEGATIVE word is more self descriptive, as their affect becomes more PLEASANT than their average, they will take relatively LONGER to make the choice. 

    - When pts are deciding between posneg wordpair pair  where the POSITIVE word is more self descriptive, as their affect becomes more PLEASANT than their average, they will take relatively SHORTER to make the choice.

- UNPLEASANT
    - When pts are deciding between posneg wordpair pair where the NEGATIVE word is more self descriptive, as their affect becomes more UNPLEASANT than their average, they will take relatively SHORTER to make the choice. 

    - When pts are deciding between posneg wordpair pair wheree the POSITIVE word is more self descriptive, as their affect becomes more UNPLEASANT than their average, they will take relatively LONGER to make the choice.
Code 
## H1a. Mood Congruency summary table

summary_table_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(affect_bin, delta_sb_bin) %>%
    summarise(
        N = n(),
        `P(Chose Pos)` = round(mean(chose_positive), 3),
        `RT Mean` = round(mean(rt), 3),
        .groups = "drop"
    )


#cat("H1a. MOOD CONGRUENCY: SUMMARY OF p(chose_pos) and RT by affect and delta_posneg_sb")
#print(summary_table_cong)

H1a. Mood Congruency: Plots

Code 
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# H1a. POS CHOICE AND RT PLOTS
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#---Plot p(chose_pos) ---#
p_choice_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(affect_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(chose_positive), se = sd(chose_positive) / sqrt(n())) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = affect_bin, y = mean, fill = affect_bin)) +
    geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#808080", "#2166AC")) +
    labs(x = "affect_z", y = "p(chose_pos)", title = "Mood Congruency: p(chose_pos) by affect(z)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

#---Plot RT ---#
p_rt_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(delta_sb_bin, affect_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(rt), se = sd(rt) / sqrt(n()), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = delta_sb_bin, y = mean, color = affect_bin, group = affect_bin)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#808080", "#2166AC")) +
    labs(
        x = "delta_posneg_sb",
        y = "RT (s)",
        color = "affect",
        title = "Mood Congruency: RT by delta_posneg_sb and affect_z"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )

p_cong_combined <- p_choice_cong + p_rt_cong +
    plot_annotation(
        title = "H1a. Mood Congruency: p(chose_pos) + RT plots",
        theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16))
    )
print(p_cong_combined)

H1a. Mood Congruency: Plots

Code 
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# H1a. POS CHOICE AND RT PLOTS
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#---Plot p(chose_pos) ---#
p_choice_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(affect_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(chose_positive), se = sd(chose_positive) / sqrt(n())) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = affect_bin, y = mean, fill = affect_bin)) +
    geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#808080", "#2166AC")) +
    labs(x = "Momentary Affect", y = "P(Choose Positive)") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

#---Plot RT ---#
p_rt_cong <- df_cong %>%
    # new 5-bin version of delta_posneg_sb so that the x axis matches the empirical figure
    mutate(delta_sb_bin_5 = cut(delta_posneg_sb,
        breaks = seq(-0.5, 0.5, by = 0.2),
        labels = c(
            "-100 to -60", "-60 to -20", "-20 to 20",
            "20 to 60", "60 to 100"
        ),
        include.lowest = TRUE
    )) %>%
    group_by(delta_sb_bin_5, affect_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(rt), se = sd(rt) / sqrt(n()), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = delta_sb_bin_5, y = mean, color = affect_bin, group = affect_bin)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#808080", "#2166AC")) +
    labs(
        x = "Δ Self-Belief (Positive word - Negative word)",
        y = "Reaction Time (s)",
        color = "Affect"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )

p_cong_combined <- p_choice_cong + p_rt_cong
print(p_cong_combined)

Classic RT and p(chose_pos) plots for Congruency (no affect bin)

Code 
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLASSIC RT AND CHOICE BY SELF BELEIF PLOTS FOR CONGRUENCY (no affect bin)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# p(chose pos) by delta_sb_bin
p_choice_by_dsb_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(delta_sb_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(chose_positive), se = sd(chose_positive) / sqrt(n()), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = delta_sb_bin, y = mean)) +
    geom_point(color = "#D35400", size = 3) +
    geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "#D35400", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2, color = "#D35400") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(y = "p(chose_pos)", x = NULL) +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())

# RT by delta_sb_bin
p_rt_by_dsb_cong <- df_cong %>%
    group_by(delta_sb_bin) %>%
    summarise(mean = mean(rt), se = sd(rt) / sqrt(n()), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = delta_sb_bin, y = mean)) +
    geom_point(color = "#D35400", size = 3) +
    geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "#D35400", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.2, color = "#D35400") +
    labs(y = "RT", x = "delta_posneg_sb") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


p_dsb_cong <- p_choice_by_dsb_cong / p_rt_by_dsb_cong +
    plot_annotation(
        title = "CONGRUENCY: p(chose pos) and RT by delta_posneg_sb",
        theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
    )
print(p_dsb_cong)

EXPORT PROCESSED DF

Code 
# define folder
processed_folder <- "processed_data"

# make it if dont exist
if (!dir.exists(processed_folder)) dir.create(processed_folder)

# build filename
filename_processed <- "processed_simulated_data"

# export
write_csv(df_cong, file.path(processed_folder, filename_processed))

————————————————————————————————

02.18.26 New Models (Single Experiments) no scaling delta_posneg sb

————————————————————————————————

Code 
#---H1a. Congruency Choice MAX---#

m_choice_cong <- glmer(
  chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb +
    (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb | subject_id),
  data = df_cong,
  family = binomial
)


#---H1a. Congruency RT MAX---#
m_rt_cong <- lmer(
  rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + 
    (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad | subject_id),
  data = df_cong
)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
#---H1a. Congruency RT UNCORRELATE RANDOM EFFECTS---#

m_choice_cong1 <- glmer(
  chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb +
    (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb || subject_id),
  data = df_cong,
  family = binomial
)

#---H1a. Congruency RT UNCORRELATE RANDOM EFFECTS---#
m_rt_cong1 <- lmer(
  rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + 
    (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad || subject_id),
  data = df_cong
)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #


m_choice_cong2 <- glmer(
  chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb +
    (1 + affect_z || subject_id),
  data = df_cong,
  family = binomial
)
#---H1a. Congruency NO QUAD RANDOM EFFECT (RT ONLY)---#

m_rt_cong2 <- lmer(
  rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + 
    (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin || subject_id),
  data = df_cong
)



# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

#---H1a. Congruency NO RANDOM EFFECT INTERACTION (RT ONLY) ---#
m_rt_cong3 <- lmer(
  rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + 
    (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb_lin || subject_id),
  data = df_cong
)




# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
cat("\n--- MAX MODELS ---\n")

--- MAX MODELS ---
Code 
summary(m_choice_cong)
Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
  Approximation) [glmerMod]
 Family: binomial  ( logit )
Formula: chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb + (1 + affect_z +  
    delta_posneg_sb | subject_id)
   Data: df_cong

      AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
  29128.4   29203.8  -14555.2   29110.4     32151 

Scaled residuals: 
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-14.9067  -0.5293   0.1757   0.5231  11.9977 

Random effects:
 Groups     Name            Variance Std.Dev. Corr       
 subject_id (Intercept)     0.4642   0.6813              
            affect_z        0.3038   0.5512    0.18      
            delta_posneg_sb 2.3086   1.5194    0.00 -0.18
Number of obs: 32160, groups:  subject_id, 70

Fixed effects:
                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)      0.51481    0.08302   6.201  5.6e-10 ***
affect_z         0.82578    0.06810  12.126  < 2e-16 ***
delta_posneg_sb  6.07845    0.19647  30.938  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Correlation of Fixed Effects:
            (Intr) affct_
affect_z     0.174       
dlt_psng_sb  0.022 -0.128

with conditional variances for "subject_id"

These seem to be the best (complex and converge + not singular) models

Code 
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
#---H1a. Congruency RT UNCORRELATE RANDOM EFFECTS---#

m_choice_cong1 <- glmer(
  chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb +
    (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb || subject_id),
  data = df_cong,
  family = binomial
)

#---H1a. Congruency RT UNCORRELATE RANDOM EFFECTS---#
m_rt_cong1 <- lmer(
  rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + 
    (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad || subject_id),
  data = df_cong
)

————————————————————————————————

Models (*100 Experiments) RUNNING THE MODELS ON 100 EXPERIMENTS

————————————————————————————————

Code 
n_cores <- detectCores()
cl <- makeCluster(floor(n_cores * 0.75))
registerDoParallel(cl)

cong_list <- df_cong_100 %>% group_split(experiment_num)


extract_effects <- function(model, model_name, error_msg) {
  # Just basically all info I can think of from model objects just in case 
  base_info <- list(
    model = model_name,
    converged = FALSE,
    singular = NA,
    conv_code = NA,
    optimizer = NA,
    feval = NA,
    max_grad = NA,
    conv_messages = NA,
    optinfo_warnings = NA,
    error_message = error_msg
  )
  
  if (is.null(model)) {
    return(data.frame(
      term = NA, estimate = NA, std.error = NA, statistic = NA,
      p.value = NA, effect_type = NA,
      group = NA, term2 = NA,
      as.data.frame(base_info),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  optinfo <- model@optinfo
  
  conv_code <- optinfo$conv$opt
  
  converged <- as.numeric(!any(grepl("failed to converge", optinfo$conv$lme4$messages, ignore.case = TRUE)))
  
  if (length(optinfo$conv$lme4$messages) > 0) {
    conv_messages <- paste(optinfo$conv$lme4$messages, collapse = "; ")
  } else {
    conv_messages <- NA
  }
  
  if (length(optinfo$warnings) > 0) {
    optinfo_warnings <- paste(optinfo$warnings, collapse = "; ")
  } else {
    optinfo_warnings <- NA
  }
  
  # pull max gradient whch is involved in likelehood/optimization
  if (!is.null(optinfo$derivs$gradient)) {
    max_grad <- max(abs(optinfo$derivs$gradient))
  } else {
    max_grad <- NA
  }
  
  model_summary <- summary(model)
  coefs <- model_summary$coefficients
  
  # Fixed effects
  fixed_coefs <- fixef(model)
  
  if ("Pr(>|z|)" %in% colnames(coefs)) {
    p_values <- coefs[, "Pr(>|z|)"]
    stat_values <- coefs[, "z value"]
  } else {
    p_values <- coefs[, "Pr(>|t|)"]
    stat_values <- coefs[, "t value"]
  }
  
    
    fixed_effects <- data.frame(
      term = names(fixed_coefs),
      estimate = coefs[, "Estimate"],
      std.error = coefs[, "Std. Error"],
      statistic = stat_values,
      p.value = p_values,
      effect_type = "fixed",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  row.names(fixed_effects) <- NULL
  
  # Random effects
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model))
  
  # Create random effects data frame
  random_effects <- data.frame(
    group = vc$grp,
    term = vc$var1,
    term2 = vc$var2,
    estimate = vc$sdcor,
    effect_type = "random",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  fixed_effects$group <- NA
  fixed_effects$term2 <- NA
  random_effects$std.error <- NA
  random_effects$statistic <- NA
  random_effects$p.value <- NA
  
  all_effects <- rbind(fixed_effects, random_effects)
  
  all_effects$model <- model_name
  all_effects$converged <- converged
  all_effects$singular <- isSingular(model)
  all_effects$conv_code <- conv_code
  all_effects$optimizer <- optinfo$optimizer
  all_effects$feval <- optinfo$feval
  all_effects$max_grad <- max_grad
  all_effects$conv_messages <- conv_messages
  all_effects$optinfo_warnings <- optinfo_warnings
  all_effects$error_message <- error_msg
  
  return(all_effects)
}

# FIT THE MODELS
fit_experiment <- function(df_cong, exp_num) {
  cat("Experiment", exp_num, "\n")
  
  # H1a. Choice
  error_choice_cong <- NA
  m_choice_cong <- tryCatch(
    glmer(
      chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb +
        (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb || subject_id),
      data = df_cong,
      family = binomial
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      error_choice_cong <<- e$message
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  # H1a. RT
  error_rt_cong <- NA
  m_rt_cong <- tryCatch(
    lmer(
      rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad +
        (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad || 
         subject_id),
      data = df_cong
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      error_rt_cong <<- e$message
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  
  
  
  res1 <- extract_effects(m_choice_cong, "choice_cong", error_choice_cong)
  res2 <- extract_effects(m_rt_cong, "rt_cong", error_rt_cong)
  
  
  all_res <- rbind(res1, res2)
  all_res$experiment <- exp_num
  
  return(all_res)
}

# Run all 
all_results <- foreach(
   #i = 1:10,
   i = seq_along(cong_list),
  .combine = rbind,
  .packages = c("lme4", "lmerTest")
) %dopar% {
  fit_experiment(
    cong_list[[i]],
    unique(cong_list[[i]]$experiment_num)
  )
}

stopCluster(cl)

————————————————————————————————

CORRECTED POWER SUMMARY

————————————————————————————————

Code 
#---POWER ANALYSIS SUMMARY 

n_total <- length(unique(all_results$experiment))

# FIXED EFFECTS
power_summary1 <- all_results %>%
  filter(effect_type == "fixed") %>%
  group_by(model, term) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n_total,
    n_converged = sum(converged == 1),
    n_singular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 1),
    n_convergednotsingular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 0),
    convergence_rate = n_converged / n_total,
    singular_rate = n_singular / n_total,
    convergednotsingular_rate = n_convergednotsingular / n_total,
    power_outof_convergednotsingular = sum(p.value < 0.05 & converged == 1 & singular == 0, na.rm = TRUE) / n_convergednotsingular,
    power_outof150 = sum(p.value < 0.05 & converged == 1 & singular == 0, na.rm = TRUE) / n_total,
    mean_estimate = mean(estimate[converged == 1 & singular == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_estimate = sd(estimate[converged == 1 & singular == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_se = mean(std.error[converged == 1 & singular == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(power_summary1, n = Inf, width = Inf)
# A tibble: 8 × 14
  model       term                         n_total n_converged n_singular

Code 
# WHAT IS SINGULAR???
singular_summary1 <- all_results %>%
  distinct(model, experiment, converged, singular) %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n_total,
    n_converged = sum(converged == 1),
    n_singular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 1),
    n_convergednotsingular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 0),
    pct_converged = (n_converged / n_total) * 100,
    pct_singular = (n_singular / n_total) * 100,
    pct_convergednotsingular = (n_convergednotsingular / n_total) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(singular_summary1, n = Inf, width = Inf)
# A tibble: 2 × 8
  model       n_total n_converged n_singular n_convergednotsingular
  <chr>         <int>       <int>      <int>                  <int>
1 choice_cong     150         150          0                    150
2 rt_cong         150         109          0                    109
  pct_converged pct_singular pct_convergednotsingular
          <dbl>        <dbl>                    <dbl>
1         100              0                    100  
2          72.7            0                     72.7
Code 
# RANDOM EFFECTS
random_summary1 <- all_results %>%
  filter(effect_type == "random") %>%
  group_by(model, term, group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n_total,
    n_converged = sum(converged == 1),
    n_singular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 1),
    n_convergednotsingular = sum(converged == 1 & singular == 0),
    mean_estimate = mean(estimate[converged == 1 & singular == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_estimate = sd(estimate[converged == 1 & singular == 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(random_summary1, n = Inf, width = Inf)

4          0                    109        0.119      0.00962
5          0                    109        0.0449     0.00986
6          0                    109        1.31       0.179  
7          0                    109        1.91       0.214  
8          0                    109        1.28       0.157  
9          0                    109        0.966      0.0151

SUMMARY OF DIRECTION AND SIGNIFICANCE OF ALL FIXED EFFECTS

Code 
# KNOW WHICH DIRECTION THE BETA OF THE FIXED EFFECTS ARE GOING AND IF SIGNIFICANT
full_summary1 <- all_results %>%
  filter(converged == 1, effect_type == "fixed") %>%
  #filter(effect_type == "fixed") %>%
  mutate(
    direction = case_when(
      estimate > 0 ~ "positive",
      estimate < 0 ~ "negative",
      TRUE ~ "zero"
    ),
    significant = p.value < 0.05
  ) %>%
  select(
    experiment,
    model,
    term,
    estimate,
    std.error,
    statistic,
    p.value,
    direction,
    significant,
    converged,
    singular
  ) %>%
  arrange(model, term, experiment)
#full_summary1

SAME AS ABOVE SUMMARY BUT INCLUDES MODEL SPECIFICATION

Code 
# LITERALLY EVERYTHING I COULD WANT TO KNOW IN A DF (BASICALLY THE ABOVE BUT WITH COL FOR THE EXACT MODEL SPECIIFCATION AND TYP OF MODEL)
mega_full_summary1 <- all_results %>%
  filter(effect_type == "fixed") %>%
  mutate(
    direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "positive", "negative"),
    significant = p.value < 0.05,
    model_formula = case_when(
        model == "choice_cong" ~ "chose_positive ~ affect_z + delta_posneg_sb + (1 + affect_z + delta_posneg_sb || subject_id)",
        model == "rt_cong" ~ "rt_z ~ affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad + (1 + affect_z * delta_posneg_sb_lin + delta_posneg_sb_quad || subject_id)"
      ),
    model_type = ifelse(grepl("choice", model), "binomial logistic(glmer)", "linearMEML(lmer)"),
    outcome = ifelse(grepl("choice", model), "chose_positive", "rt_z"),
    condition = ifelse(grepl("incong", model), "incongruent", "congruent")
  ) %>%
  select(
    experiment,
    model,
    condition,
    model_type,
    outcome,
    model_formula,
    term,
    estimate,
    std.error,
    statistic,
    p.value,
    direction,
    significant,
    converged,
    singular
  ) %>%
  arrange(model, term, experiment)
#mega_full_summary1

#write.csv(mega_full_summary1, "power_analysis_full_results1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

——————————————————————————————————————————————

BASICALLY OUR MOST IMPORTANT EFFECTS AND IF THEY ARE IN DIRECTION WITH OUR HYPOTHESES

——————————————————————————————————————————————

Code 
power_results1 <- mega_full_summary1 %>%
  filter(converged == 1, singular == 0) %>%
  mutate(
    expected_dir = case_when(
      # H1a CONGRUENCY: CHOICE: based on hypotheses.. beta for affect_z should be POSITIVE
      model == "choice_cong" & term == "affect_z" ~ "positive",
      # H1a CONGRUENCY: RT: beta for affect_z:delta_posneg_sb should be NEGATIVE
      model == "rt_cong" & term == "affect_z:delta_posneg_sb_lin" ~ "negative",
      # H1b INCONGRUENCY: CHOICE: beta for affect_z_transform_emalevel should be POSITIVE
      model == "choice_incong" & term == "affect_z_transform_emalevel" ~ "positive",
      # H1b INCONGRUENCY: RT: beta for affect_z_transform_emalevel:delta_posneg_sb should be NEGATIVE
      model == "rt_incong" & term == "affect_z_transform_emalevel:delta_posneg_sb_lin" ~ "negative",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    sig_correct_dir = case_when(
      expected_dir == "positive" ~ p.value < 0.05 & estimate > 0,
      expected_dir == "negative" ~ p.value < 0.05 & estimate < 0,
      TRUE ~ NA)) %>% 
  group_by(model, term, expected_dir) %>%
  summarise(
    n_convergednotsingular = n(),
    n_total = n_total,
    k = sum(sig_correct_dir, na.rm = TRUE),
    power_outof_convergednotsingular = k / n_convergednotsingular, # k is number of models where the effect was significant and in correct direction  
    power_outof150 = k / n_total,
    mean_b = mean(estimate),
    .groups = "drop")

print(power_results1, n = Inf, width = Inf) # k is number of models where the effect was significant and in correct direction  
# A tibble: 8 × 9
  model       term                         expected_dir n_convergednotsingular
  <chr>       <chr>                        <chr>                         <int>
1 choice_cong (Intercept)                  <NA>                            150
2 choice_cong affect_z                     positive                        150
3 choice_cong delta_posneg_sb              <NA>                            150
4 rt_cong     (Intercept)                  <NA>                            109
5 rt_cong     affect_z                     <NA>                            109
6 rt_cong     affect_z:delta_posneg_sb_lin negative                        109
7 rt_cong     delta_posneg_sb_lin          <NA>                            109
8 rt_cong     delta_posneg_sb_quad         <NA>                            109
  n_total     k power_outof_convergednotsingular power_outof150    mean_b
    <int> <int>                            <dbl>          <dbl>     <dbl>
1     150     0                                0          0      0.594   
2     150   150                                1          1      0.729   
3     150     0                                0          0      6.24    
4     150     0                                0          0     -0.000594
5     150     0                                0          0     -0.0292  
6     150   109                                1          0.727 -1.93    
7     150     0                                0          0     -1.80    
8     150     0                                0          0     -2.92
